questions.question: Define Digital Logic Design and explain its importance in modern computing.
keyPoints: Digital Logic Design is the design of digital circuits using binary digits (0 and 1)
It forms the foundation of all modern computing devices
Essential for understanding how computers and electronic devices work
Enables creation of processors, memory, and all digital systems

questions.question: Explain the difference between Positive Logic and Negative Logic conventions.
keyPoints: Positive Logic: Higher voltage = 1 (TRUE), Lower voltage = 0 (FALSE)
Negative Logic: Higher voltage = 0 (FALSE), Lower voltage = 1 (TRUE)
Positive logic is more commonly used in digital systems
Negative logic inverts the voltage-to-binary mapping

questions.question: What are the three basic logic gates? Describe each briefly.
keyPoints: AND Gate: Output is 1 only when all inputs are 1
OR Gate: Output is 1 when at least one input is 1
NOT Gate (Inverter): Output is complement of input (inverts 0 to 1 and 1 to 0)

questions.question: Define Boolean Algebra and explain its role in Digital Logic Design.
keyPoints: Boolean Algebra is a mathematical system using binary variables (0 and 1)
Uses operators: AND (.), OR (+), NOT (')
Used to represent and simplify logical expressions
Forms the mathematical foundation for designing digital circuits

questions.question: What is the difference between Combinational and Sequential logic circuits?
keyPoints: Combinational: Output depends only on current inputs (no memory)
Sequential: Output depends on current inputs AND past inputs (has memory)
Sequential circuits use storage elements like flip-flops
Examples: Adders are combinational, counters are sequential

questions.question: List any four advantages of digital systems over analog systems.
keyPoints: Greater accuracy and precision
Immunity to noise and signal degradation
Easier to design, implement, and troubleshoot
Data can be stored and transmitted easily without loss
Programmable and flexible functionality

questions.question: What is a Truth Table? How many rows would a truth table have for a circuit with 3 inputs?
keyPoints: Truth Table lists all possible input combinations with their corresponding outputs
For n inputs, truth table has 2^n rows
Complete functional specification of a digital circuit

questions.question: Explain what Digital Logic Design is, discuss its significance in modern electronics, and describe the basic components used in digital circuit design.
keyPoints: Digital Logic Design: design of circuits using binary digits 0 and 1
Significance: Foundation of all computers, smartphones, embedded systems
Basic components: Logic gates (AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR)
Boolean algebra for expression representation and simplification
Truth tables for functional specification
Combinational and Sequential circuit categories
Applications: Processors, memory, communication systems, control systems

questions.question: Define and explain the three basic logic operations (AND, OR, NOT) with their truth tables and standard symbols. Also explain what is meant by a 'Universal Gate'.
keyPoints: AND: Output = 1 only when all inputs are 1; Symbol: D-shape
OR: Output = 1 when at least one input is 1; Symbol: curved shield
NOT: Inverts input; Output = complement of input; Symbol: triangle with circle
Universal Gate: NAND or NOR gate can implement any Boolean function
NAND is 'universal' because any logic function can be built using only NAND gates
Provide complete truth tables for each gate

questions.question: Discuss the advantages of digital systems over analog systems. Also explain the concept of binary representation in digital electronics and why binary is preferred over other number systems.
keyPoints: Advantages: Noise immunity, accuracy, flexibility, programmability, easier storage/transmission
Binary preferred: Only two voltage levels needed, easy electronic implementation
Transistors work as simple on/off switches for 0 and 1
Simpler circuit design, less susceptible to component variations
Clear threshold levels allow reliable signal detection

questions.question: What are Combinational and Sequential logic circuits? Explain with examples and discuss how they differ in terms of memory and application.
keyPoints: Combinational: Output depends only on current inputs; No memory element; Examples: Adders, Multiplexers, Decoders
Sequential: Output depends on current inputs AND stored state; Contains memory elements; Examples: Counters, Registers, Shift Registers
Sequential circuits use flip-flops or latches as storage elements
Combinational used for arithmetic/logic operations; Sequential used for memory, timing, state machines
Timing behavior: Combinational is instantaneous; Sequential depends on clock
